assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and can receive email when the selected route requires it.
No platform account, tenant, credentials, or product resources exist yet.

developer_goal: Reach first Semgrep scan success, resolving whether Community Edition no-account CLI or AppSec Platform cloud onboarding is the selected route.

first_success.outcome: Either Semgrep CE reports a successful sample scan finding, or AppSec Platform CLI login completes a first project scan, depending on route.
first_success.observable_signal: Terminal shows Scan completed successfully or semgrep ci finishes for the selected route.

route_selection.surface: Semgrep Community Edition quickstart versus Semgrep AppSec Platform quickstart
route_selection.rule: Reconstruct complete official candidate routes, prefer no payment/admin/approval when possible, then minimize required actions; leave consequential ties unresolved.

candidates.name: Semgrep Community Edition no-account Python sample scan
candidates.reason: No-account path may win lexicographically; OS/package-manager variants still need a fixed install route.

candidates.name: Semgrep AppSec Platform GitHub/GitLab signup then semgrep ci
candidates.reason: Requires org creation and IdP permissions; different product surface and terminal.

required_path.action: Create a Semgrep AppSec Platform organization if the cloud route is selected.
required_path.observable_result: A Semgrep organization exists and CLI scan instructions are shown.

required_path.action: Install Semgrep CLI for the selected OS route.

required_fields.label: Install command
required_fields.notes: pipx, uv, brew, and OS-specific variants are peers.

required_path.action: Run the selected first scan command.

required_fields.label: semgrep scan or semgrep ci command

required_path.observable_result: Scan completes with the documented success signal for the selected route.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.10+ is required where the AppSec Platform CLI route runs.
Community Edition quickstart has its own OS prerequisites.

external_gates.description: GitHub or GitLab OAuth permissions for the AppSec Platform route.

platform_outcomes.description: Semgrep reports findings or a successful scan completion message.

excluded.item: Homebrew install when using preferred pipx or uv on macOS AppSec route
excluded.reason: Docs mark Homebrew as best-effort and often lagging.

sources.title: Get started with Semgrep Community Edition
Quickstart
evidence_supported: AppSec Platform signup and CLI scan

uncertainties.question: Should first success be Community Edition no-account CLI or AppSec Platform cloud onboarding?
uncertainties.impact: These routes differ on account creation, IdP gates, and scan commands.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Explicit corpus selection of one Semgrep first-mile product surface.

uncertainties.question: Which install method and OS should be fixed for the selected route?
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Pick one OS and preferred installer, then verify against current docs.
